Interface AppContext

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultAppContext
public interface AppContext extends AutoCloseable
Represents a lightweight runtime environment for an application. Contains services such as graphics, audio, input, and asset management.

Registered services are also called "components" or "beans" in documentation of this interface's methods.

See Also:
  • AppContextHolder
  • Global

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Closes this context and releases beans that implement AutoCloseable.
    int
    count(Class<?> type)
    Returns the count of beans registered under the given type.

    Gets a bean instance by its type (usually an interface type).

    Gets all registered beans by the given type.

    Determines whether there is at least one bean registered under the given type.

    Registers a lazy-initialized component under the given type.
    <T> AppContext
    registerOverride(Class<T> type, T instance)
    Registers a new component under the given type, guaranteeing that get(Class) returns that component instance after call to this method.

  • Method Details

    • get

      <T> T get(Class<T> type)
      Gets a bean instance by its type (usually an interface type). There's no guarantee to which bean will be returned if multiple beans were registered under that type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the bean
      Parameters:
      type - the type that was used to register the bean
      Returns:
      the bean instance
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no beans were registered under the given type
      NullPointerException - if type is null, or if there's a lazy bean registered under that type and its factory function returns null
      See Also:
      • getAll(Class)

    • getAll

      <T> Collection<T> getAll(Class<T> type)
      Gets all registered beans by the given type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the common supertype of the beans
      Parameters:
      type - the type
      Returns:
      all beans registered under that type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no beans were registered under the given type
      NullPointerException - if type is null, or if there's a lazy bean registered under that type and its factory function returns null
      See Also:
      • get(Class)

    • count

      int count(Class<?> type)
      Returns the count of beans registered under the given type.

      This method doesn't initialize lazy beans, so it can be used instead of getAll(type).size() to avoid initialization when only the count of them is needed.

      Parameters:
      type - the type
      Returns:
      the count of beans registered under the given type
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type is null

    • has

      default boolean has(Class<?> type)
      Determines whether there is at least one bean registered under the given type. Returns true if so and false otherwise.

      This method doesn't initialize lazy beans, so it can be used instead of !getAll(type).isEmpty() to avoid initialization when only needed to check presence.

      Implementations may choose to return false instead of throwing a NullPointerException when the given type is null.

      Parameters:
      type - the type
      Returns:
      whether any beans are registered under the given type
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - (optional) if type is null

    • register

      <T> AppContext register(Class<T> type, T instance)
      Registers a new component under the given type. Multiple components can be registered under the same type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      instance - the component instance to register
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      See Also:
      • registerLazy(Class, Function)
      • registerOverride(Class, Object)

    • registerOverride

      <T> AppContext registerOverride(Class<T> type, T instance)
      Registers a new component under the given type, guaranteeing that get(Class) returns that component instance after call to this method.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      instance - the component instance to register, overriding other instances with the same type
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      See Also:
      • registerLazy(Class, Function)

    • registerLazy

      <T> AppContext registerLazy(Class<T> type, Function<? super AppContext, ? extends T> factory)
      Registers a lazy-initialized component under the given type. Lazy components are not initialized until they're requested with get(Class) or similar methods.

      This is useful for registering heavy beans that might never be used, or beans that do some irreversible changes once created.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      factory - a factory function that accepts this context and produces a new component instance
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null (does not check for the factory's return value)
      See Also:
      • registerLazy(Class, Supplier)

    • registerLazy

      default <T> AppContext registerLazy(Class<T> type, Supplier<? extends T> factory)
      Registers a lazy-initialized component under the given type. Lazy components are not initialized until they're requested with get(Class) or similar methods.

      This is useful for registering heavy beans that might never be used, or beans that do some irreversible changes once created.

      This method is a version of registerLazy(Class, Function) where the factory function does not accept arguments.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      factory - a factory function that produces a new component instance
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null (does not check for the factory's return value)
      See Also:
      • registerLazy(Class, Function)

    • close

      void close() throws Exception
      Closes this context and releases beans that implement AutoCloseable. The context cannot be used after calling this method. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
